title: podman pause container from registry.redhat.io?
body:
/kind feature

**Description**

why isnt pause pulled from registry.redhat.io if im logged in?  
if "The Red Hat Ecosystem Catalog is the official source for discovering and learning more about the Red Hat Ecosystem of both Red Hat and certified third-party products and services." then why am I pulling from Googles registry too?

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.podman pod create --name foo 

2. watch things fail

**Describe the results you received:**

ERRO[0001] Error freeing pod lock after failed creation: no such file or directory 
Error: unable to create pod: error adding Infra Container: unable to pull k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.1: unable to pull image: Error initializing source docker://k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.1: error pinging docker registry k8s.gcr.io

**Describe the results you expected:**
pull that pod from registry.redhat.io, the official source for discovering and learning more about the Red Hat Ecosystem of both Red Hat and certified third-party products and services.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

pulling from a single registry that bills itself as "Enterprise-Grade Containers"
and "Your trusted source for secure, certified, and up-to-date container images" 
is an excellent way to improve auditability of dockerless rootless containers in an 
enterprise setting.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.6.4
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.12
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64

```

enterprise environment with boring enterprise requirements.

comments.body: There has been talk around this - let me see if I can dig up details
This seems like a Red Hat specific request, which should go through Red Hat customer channels rather than through the vendor-neutral upstream here :)

Ran into an issue with this today. Podman 1.6.4

My organization blocks storage.googleapis.com. OpenDNS was hijacking the HTTP request to show a content filtering error page. 

```
[root ~]# podman pod create --name my_pod
ERRO[0001] Error freeing pod lock after failed creation: no such file or directory 
Error: unable to create pod: error adding Infra Container: unable to pull k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.1: unable to pull image: Error parsing image configuration: Get https://storage.googleapis.com/us.artifacts.google-containers.appspot.com/containers/images/sha256:da86e6ba6ca197bf6bc5e9d900febd906b133eaa4750e6bed647b0fbe50ed43e: remote error: tls: handshake failure
```

If you google [storage.googleapis.com](https://www.google.com/search?client=ubuntu&channel=fs&q=storage.googleapis.com) the top results are mostly about it being a malicious site. It's heavy handed security policy, but it's probably a not-uncommon filter on corporate networks.
Is it safe for podman to check registries.conf for pause container location as well?
will be enough on RHEL to change the default setting for `infra_image` in `libpod.conf`?

> @vrothberg
> there sure are a lot of Red Hat specific members in "the containers organzation," so you'll have to forgive me if I get my issues misplaced ;)

Fully understand why you opened the issue :)

> Opened case 02585119 for the full enterprise experience.

Thanks!

Since it's a Red Hat specific request, I am closing the issue. As @giuseppe mentioned above, the infra image can be changed in the `libpod.conf` if desired.
Which means this is a packaging issue and should be handled by a Red Hat bugzilla.
